codeReview.summary: Reviewed the feature definition, handoff, transcript skeleton, commit `0f8b3ee4480885306977832351478b6313d2c387`, and the current branch state for the AI SDK refactor. The file-splitting itself is mostly behavior-preserving, but I found one real compatibility regression in message normalization plus a blocking scope violation from bundling release version bumps into a non-release refactor commit.

issues.description: `normalizeToolCalls()` no longer preserves every tool-call payload shape that the pre-refactor code accepted. The old implementation handled top-level `arguments` plus `func`/`tool` aliases, but the refactored logic only reads `call.args` and `call.function.arguments`. `Message` still advertises those legacy shapes in `packages/extension/ai/message-types.ts`, so histories/provider payloads using them now lose tool names or arguments during normalization, violating the feature's `Message conversion preserves content` requirement. Evidence: current `message-factory.ts:136-139` versus the pre-refactor `message-schema.ts` implementation at historical line 197 in commit `0f8b3ee^`.

The feature commit bundled unrelated release metadata changes by bumping `package.json:3`, `packages/extension/manifest.json:4`, and `packages/extension/manifest.firefox.json:15` from `0.4.63` to `0.4.64`. Mission guidance and the refactor-worker skill explicitly forbid version bumps in non-release refactors, so this is a blocking scope violation even if later commits changed the versions again.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission and skill both treat `npm run check:repo-standards` as evidence that refactored files meet the 200-line limit, but the actual guardrail still defaults to 300 lines. That mismatch makes workers over-report compliance unless they separately run `wc -l` checks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md lines 9, 13, 39, and 182 all state a 200-line hard limit; the reviewed worker transcript for session `9df0cc80-1e46-42ca-a3fc-549f2dece590` records `repo-standards: pass (base=1f6d631bfda7, maxLines=300, filesChecked=229)`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared architecture notes still describe `packages/extension/ai/` only as a single bucket and do not record that `sdk-client.ts`, `message-schema.ts`, `model-convert.ts`, and `providers/registry.ts` are now thin barrel files over focused helper modules. Future workers are likely to inspect or edit the barrels first and miss the real implementation files.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md:85` only lists `ai/               # AI/LLM integration`, while current files such as `packages/extension/ai/sdk-client.ts` and `packages/extension/ai/message-schema.ts` are barrel exports delegating to split helper modules.

summary: Fail. The AI SDK refactor mostly preserves structure, but it introduced a tool-call normalization regression in `message-factory.ts` and the feature commit improperly bundled version bumps in `package.json` and both extension manifests. I also noted shared-state gaps around the real repo-standards line limit and the lack of library notes for the new AI module barrel/helper layout.
